DOES_NOT_OBSERVE: modules loaded by child processes the command spawns
browser-side execution of the built bundle
packages loaded by build phases outside this window
scope_warning: this artifact covers the node runtime ONLY. Absence of a package here is NOT evidence about the python runtime, and vice versa.
assurance_lane: BUILD/DEV — vite and esbuild are devDependencies and do not execute inside a deployed HELM artifact
label: vite_build_POST_patch
method: node --require (CJS Module._load) + --import module.register (ESM resolve); no package patched
hook_api_note: module.register() is used here. Newer Node exposes module.registerHooks() as a synchronous alternative; the observer should stay abstracted from the hook API so a Node upgrade does not silently reduce coverage — the failure mode would look exactly like a clean result.

vite.why_watched: dev-server CVEs: server.fs.deny bypass, path traversal in optimized deps

esbuild.why_watched: dev server accepts any-origin requests and returns the response

claim_this_supports: OBSERVED_ABSENT_IN_THIS_RUN for the not-loaded set — NOT 'unreachable'. One window is one observation, in one runtime.
